criteria: The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand the two-stage pipeline for personality-guided code generation, including the personality generation and code generation components.
The agent has read Figure 1 to understand the workflow and the specific prompt templates used for personality generation and code generation.
The agent has read Section 4.2 to understand the four datasets: MBPP Sanitized (427 tasks), MBPP+, HumanEval+ (164 tasks), and APPS (500 randomly sampled interview-level problems).
The agent has read Section 4.3 and Table 1 to understand the seven LLMs used: GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, Llama3.1 (70B), Qwen-Long, DeepSeek-Coder V2 (236B), Codestral (22B), and CodeLlama (13B).
The agent has read Section 4.4 to understand the evaluation metric: pass rate averaged over three runs, as defined in Equation 1.
The agent has read Appendix A to understand the exact prompt templates for personality generation, code generation, few-shot learning, and CoT prompting.
The agent has read Table 2 to check the main RQ1 results comparing pass rates with and without personality guidance across all 7 LLMs and 4 datasets.
The agent has read Table 3 to check the RQ2 uniform MBTI experiment results for Qwen-Long on MBPP Sanitized.
The agent has read Figure 2 to check the distribution of MBTI types generated by GPT-4o for each of the four datasets.
The agent has read Figure 3 to check the Venn diagram showing the percentages of tasks solved by INTJ versus ISTJ types.
The agent has read Table 4 to check the RQ3 results comparing personality guidance with few-shot and CoT strategies and their combinations.
The agent has read Table 5 to check the RQ4 results comparing the full MBTI description (Full Prompt) versus MBTI type only (Short Prompt).
The agent has read Table 6 to check the RQ5 results comparing MBTI-based versus Big Five personality-based prompting across all 7 LLMs.
The agent has read Appendix B and Table 7 to check the comparison between using GPT-4o for personality generation versus using each LLM to generate its own personality.
The agent has read Appendix C and Table 8 to check the single-dimension ablation results for using only the S/N (Sensing/Intuition) MBTI dimension versus the full MBTI type.
The agent has read Appendix D and Table 9 to check the Pass@k evaluation results for k in {1, 5, 10} across 5 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The plan includes the two-stage pipeline: (1) personality generation using GPT-4o with the MBTI framework, and (2) code generation using LLMs with the generated MBTI personality.

The plan includes the personality generation prompt: prompting GPT-4o to generate both the MBTI type and a detailed description tailored to the specific coding task, without giving a direct answer to the problem.
The plan includes the code generation prompt: instructing the target LLM to act as a programmer with the specified MBTI personality description and generate the function code for the coding task.
The plan includes loading and processing all four datasets: MBPP Sanitized (427 tasks), MBPP+, HumanEval+ (164 tasks), and APPS (500 problems randomly sampled from the interview-level subset).
The plan includes integrating all seven LLMs for code generation: GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, Llama3.1 (70B), Qwen-Long, DeepSeek-Coder V2 (236B), Codestral (22B), and CodeLlama (13B).
The plan includes running each LLM on each dataset three times and computing the average pass rate as the evaluation metric, following Equation 1.
The plan includes the RQ1 experiment: comparing direct prompting (Direct) versus MBTI-guided code generation (MBTI) for all 7 LLMs across all 4 datasets.
The plan includes the RQ2 uniform MBTI experiment: assigning each of the 16 MBTI personality types uniformly to all tasks and evaluating Qwen-Long on MBPP Sanitized.
The plan includes the RQ3 combination experiments: testing three-shot learning, CoT, three-shot+personality, and CoT+personality for all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.

The plan includes the RQ4 prompt design experiment: comparing the full MBTI description (Full Prompt) versus MBTI type label only (Short Prompt) for all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized, and additionally comparing against a general MBTI template for Qwen-Long.
The plan includes the RQ5 personality modeling experiment: comparing MBTI-based personality prompting with Big Five personality-based prompting across all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The plan includes the Appendix B experiment: comparing GPT-4o-generated personalities versus self-generated personalities (each code LLM generates its own), excluding CodeLlama, on MBPP Sanitized.
The plan includes the Appendix C single-dimension ablation: prompting LLMs with only the S/N (Sensing/Intuition) property rather than the full MBTI type for all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The plan includes the Appendix D Pass@k evaluation for k in {1, 5, 10} for the 5 supported LLMs (GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, Llama3.1, Codestral, CodeLlama) on MBPP Sanitized.

The personality generation prompt for GPT-4o has been implemented exactly as described: asking GPT-4o to generate the MBTI type and a detailed personality description suited for the coding task, without directly answering the problem.

The code generation prompt has been implemented as described: instructing the target LLM to act as a programmer with the specified MBTI personality description, specifying the function name and the problem statement.
The direct prompting baseline has been implemented: prompting the LLM to generate code as a programmer without any personality guidance.
The pass rate formula has been implemented as Equation 1: P = (1/3) * sum_{i=1}^{3} (c_i / cnt), computing the average pass rate across three independent runs.
The test-case evaluation logic has been implemented: a generated function is considered to 'pass' only when it produces correct outputs for all test cases in the test suite.
The code for loading and processing the MBPP Sanitized dataset (427 crowd-sourced Python problems with task descriptions and automated test cases) has been implemented.
The code for loading and processing the MBPP+ dataset has been implemented.
The code for loading and processing the HumanEval+ dataset (164 manually curated Python problems with function signatures, docstrings, and unit tests) has been implemented.
The code for loading and sampling the APPS dataset has been implemented: randomly sampling 500 problems from the interview-level subset of the full 10K problem set.
The GPT-4o API integration for personality generation has been implemented, capable of receiving a coding problem and returning both the MBTI type and its detailed description.
The GPT-4o and GPT-4o mini API integrations for code generation have been implemented.
The Llama3.1 (70B) integration for code generation has been implemented.
The Qwen-Long integration for code generation has been implemented.
The DeepSeek-Coder V2 (236B) integration for code generation has been implemented.
The Codestral (22B) integration for code generation has been implemented.
The CodeLlama (13B) integration for code generation has been implemented.
The three-shot learning code generation prompt has been implemented: prepending three coding examples alongside the MBTI personality description and the task description.

The Chain of Thought (CoT) code generation prompt has been implemented: instructing the LLM with the MBTI personality to first write a rough problem-solving process and then output the final code, appending 'Let's think step by step'.

The uniform MBTI assignment experiment has been implemented: for each of the 16 MBTI personality types, prompting Qwen-Long to adopt that uniform personality type for all coding tasks on MBPP Sanitized.
The MBTI type distribution analysis has been implemented: recording and tallying the MBTI types generated by GPT-4o for each task across all four datasets to compute distribution statistics.

The short prompt variant has been implemented: replacing the full MBTI description in the code generation prompt with only the MBTI type label (e.g., 'INTJ'), keeping all other prompt components identical.

The general MBTI template experiment has been implemented: using 16 fixed, pre-generated general MBTI descriptions (generated once by GPT-4) instead of task-specific descriptions, tested with Qwen-Long on MBPP Sanitized.

The Big Five personality generation prompt for GPT-4o has been implemented: prompting GPT-4o to generate Big Five personality traits (openness, conscientiousness, extroversion, agreeableness, neuroticism) tailored to each coding task.

The Big Five personality-guided code generation prompt has been implemented: instructing LLMs to act as a programmer with the generated Big Five personality description, using the same structural format as the MBTI-based prompt.

The self-personality-generation experiment has been implemented: using each code-generation LLM (excluding CodeLlama) to generate its own MBTI personality for each task, then using that personality for code generation.

The single S/N dimension ablation has been implemented: prompting LLMs with only the Sensing (S) or Intuition (N) property determined by GPT-4o for each task, instead of the full MBTI type, on MBPP Sanitized.

The Pass@k evaluation has been implemented: for each task, generating k independent code solutions per LLM and counting the task as solved if any of the k solutions passes all test cases, with k in {1, 5, 10}.
The RQ1 experiments have been executed successfully: running direct prompting and MBTI-guided code generation for all 7 LLMs on all 4 datasets, each configuration repeated 3 times.
The RQ2 uniform MBTI experiment has been executed successfully: running all 16 MBTI personality types uniformly for Qwen-Long on MBPP Sanitized (3 runs each type).
The RQ3 combination experiments have been executed successfully: running three-shot, CoT, three-shot+personality, and CoT+personality conditions for all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The RQ4 prompt design experiments have been executed successfully: running full MBTI description, short MBTI type-only prompt, and general MBTI template conditions for all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The RQ5 personality modeling experiments have been executed successfully: running MBTI-based and Big Five-based personality prompting for all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The Appendix B experiments have been executed successfully: comparing GPT-4o-generated personalities versus self-generated personalities for 5 LLMs (excluding CodeLlama) on MBPP Sanitized.
The Appendix C single S/N dimension ablation has been executed successfully: running S/N-only personality prompting for all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The Appendix D Pass@k experiments have been executed successfully: generating k=5 and k=10 independent code solutions per task for the 5 supported LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: the personality-guided approach (MBTI) improves pass rates in 23 out of 28 LLM-dataset combinations compared to direct prompting.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: in 11 LLM-dataset combinations the improvement exceeds 5%, in 5 it surpasses 10%, and the highest gain is 12.9% for GPT-4o mini on MBPP Sanitized.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: all 7 LLMs show positive average improvements, with Qwen-Long achieving the highest average gain of 6.5% and Llama3.1 and Codestral each at 5.3%.

The execution outcomes match Table 2: GPT-4o and DeepSeek-Coder V2 (strongest models) show the smallest average improvements (1.2% and 0.6% respectively), with pass rate decreases observed on HumanEval+ and APPS.

The execution outcomes match Table 3: all 16 uniform MBTI types produce pass rates between 65.7% and 68.4% for Qwen-Long on MBPP Sanitized, all near the direct prompting rate of 68.4%, while the diverse MBTI approach achieves 80.8%.
The execution outcomes match Figure 2: INTJ dominates as the most frequently generated personality type across all four datasets (74.2%–90.6%), with ISTJ as the second most common and INTP rare.
The execution outcomes match Figure 3: approximately 62.1% of tasks on MBPP Sanitized are solvable by either INTJ or ISTJ, while 4.5% are uniquely solvable by INTJ and 4.5% only by ISTJ.
The execution outcomes match Table 4: the personality-guided approach consistently outperforms both three-shot learning and CoT strategies used alone across all 7 LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.
The execution outcomes match Table 4: CoT+Personality achieves better results than personality alone in 5 out of 7 LLMs, with improvements over direct prompting ranging from 6.3% to 13.8%.

The execution outcomes match Table 5: the full MBTI description (Full Prompt) consistently outperforms the short MBTI type-only prompt (Short Prompt) across all 7 LLMs, with an average improvement of 3.94% for Full Prompt over Short Prompt.
The execution outcomes match Table 5: the short MBTI type-only prompt still outperforms direct prompting in 6 out of 7 LLMs, confirming some benefit from even minimal personality specification.
The execution outcomes match Table 6: MBTI-based personality prompting outperforms Big Five personality prompting across all 7 evaluated LLMs on MBPP Sanitized.

The execution outcomes match Table 7: using GPT-4o consistently for personality generation outperforms self-generation in 4 out of 5 LLMs, with an average improvement of 4.1% for the remaining four LLMs.

The execution outcomes match Table 8: using only the S/N MBTI dimension improves pass rates for 4 out of 7 LLMs with an average improvement of 0.3%, while full MBTI prompting achieves a 9.5% average improvement.

The execution outcomes match Table 9: Pass@5 and Pass@10 results for the personality-guided approach consistently outperform direct prompting across all 5 tested LLMs on MBPP Sanitized, confirming the RQ1 trends.
